dotStep.description:
Step (in percent of the range) between clickable dots when
`variant="dotted"`. Defaults to `25` → stops at 0/25/50/75/100.

widgets-modal.name: Modal

stories.name: Open
stories.snippet:
const Open = () => <ModalV2 isOpen onDismiss={fn()} closeOnOverlayClick>
    <Modal title="Modal title" onDismiss={fn()}>
        <Flex flexDirection="column" style={{ gap: 12, minWidth: 320, maxWidth: 420 }}>
            <Text>Modal body content lives here. Pass anything as children.</Text>
            <Button scale="md" onClick={fn()}>OK
                          </Button>
        </Flex>
    </Modal>
</ModalV2>;
stories.description: Static, always-open for layout review in the storybook canvas.

stories.name: Interactive
stories.description: The usage pattern a consumer will typically need: parent state drives `isOpen`, a trigger button opens the modal, dismissal flips it back.

stories.name: With Back Button
stories.snippet:
const WithBackButton = () => <ModalV2 isOpen onDismiss={fn()}>
    <Modal title="Step 2 of 3" onDismiss={fn()} onBack={fn()}>
        <Flex flexDirection="column" style={{ gap: 12, minWidth: 320, maxWidth: 420 }}>
            <Text>The ← arrow appears because `onBack` is defined.</Text>
        </Flex>
    </Modal>
</ModalV2>;
stories.description: Back-button slot — pass `onBack` to render an arrow in the header for multi-step flows (e.g. the deposit wizard).

widgets-modal.import: import { Button, Flex, Modal, ModalV2, Text } from "@pancakeswap/storybook";
widgets-modal.description: The PCS `Modal` is the titled chrome (header + close button + body slot). The `ModalV2` wrapper owns the overlay, portal, and animation — its `isOpen` prop is controlled by the consumer. Every story here renders the `ModalV2` + `Modal` pair together, which is how downstream widgets (LeverageModal, DepositModal, …) compose them.

widgets-book-trades-panel-🆕.description: Tabbed container for Order Book + Recent Trades — one panel, one header. The two child slots are always mounted (hidden via `display: none` rather than conditional rendering) so live WS subscriptions inside them don't tear down on every tab toggle.

reactDocgen.description:
Tabbed container for Order Book + Recent Trades — one panel, one
header. The two child slots are always mounted (hidden via
`display: none` rather than conditional rendering) so live WS
subscriptions inside them don't tear down on every tab toggle.

bookContent.description:
Order-book content. Both slots are kept mounted; the inactive one is
hidden via `display: none` so live WS subscriptions don't tear down
on each tab switch and the trades ring buffer keeps filling while
the user is on the Book tab.

widgets-bunny-slider-🆕.description: Bunny Slider — single, unified slider replicating Figma 11:2253. Layout: a 48px tall track that fills its parent's width. • Bunny butt sits fixed at the left start • Teal middle body fills from butt to current value • Bunny head thumb follows the value • A transparent native <input type="range"> drives interaction

reactDocgen.description:
Bunny Slider — single, unified slider replicating Figma 11:2253.

  Layout: a 48px tall track that fills its parent's width.
  • Bunny butt sits fixed at the left start
  • Teal middle body fills from butt to current value
  • Bunny head thumb follows the value
  • A transparent native <input type="range"> drives interaction

valueLabel.description: Optional label rendered under the thumb (e.g. "12x", "MAX").

width.description: Forwarded to the wrapper for sizing.

widgets-chart-panel-🆕.description: Stylesheet shell for the perps chart. Owns the panel framing (border, background, min-height) so visual updates flow through this file; the actual chart implementation is provided by the consumer via `children` (TradingView paid library in pancake-frontend, or whatever else upstream wants).

reactDocgen.description:
Stylesheet shell for the perps chart. Owns the panel framing
(border, background, min-height) so visual updates flow through this
file; the actual chart implementation is provided by the consumer
via `children` (TradingView paid library in pancake-frontend, or
whatever else upstream wants).

children.description:
The actual chart widget. The consumer plugs in its
TradingView / lightweight-charts / etc. implementation here so
this widget stays free of any chart-library dependency.

minHeight.description:
Minimum height for the chart area. Defaults to 420px (matches the
pancake-frontend perps page). Pass a string ("60vh") or number
(pixels). The panel grows to fill remaining space if the parent
uses flex.

widgets-leverage-modal-🆕.import: import { Button, LeverageModal, Message, MessageText } from "@pancakeswap/storybook";

currentLeverage.description: Initial leverage shown when the modal opens.

availableBalance.description:
USDT (or quote) balance available for new positions. Used to display
the "Maximum position at current leverage" preview line.

onConfirm.description:
Called when the user clicks Confirm with their chosen leverage. The
consumer is responsible for the async write back to the venue, error
handling, and closing the modal (via `isOpen=false`) on success.

isSubmitting.description: Disables the confirm button (e.g. while the consumer's mutation is in-flight).

errorSlot.description:
Optional content slot rendered above the Confirm button. Use for
caller-classified error messages, info hints, or warnings.

tsType.name: signature
tsType.raw: (key: string, options?: Record<string, string | number | undefined>) => string

t.description:
Translator signature matches PancakeSwap's `@pancakeswap/localization`
`TranslateFunction` (data values are `string | number | undefined`) so
pancake-frontend can pass its `t` directly without a cast. Storybook
stories that don't need i18n can omit this prop — `defaultT` handles
`%placeholder%` substitution locally.

widgets-markets-dropdown-🆕.description: Markets picker dropdown - tabs for All / Favorites, text search, sorted table with live 24h stats. Stateless apart from the search query and active tab (pure view-state). Sort order: markets are rendered in the order the consumer provides. Frontend sorts by 24h quote volume desc at the hook level - matches Aster's default ordering. If you need a different order (e.g. stable alpha for storybook stories), sort before passing in.

reactDocgen.description:
Markets picker dropdown - tabs for All / Favorites, text search,
sorted table with live 24h stats. Stateless apart from the search
query and active tab (pure view-state).

Sort order: markets are rendered in the order the consumer provides.
Frontend sorts by 24h quote volume desc at the hook level - matches
Aster's default ordering. If you need a different order (e.g. stable
alpha for storybook stories), sort before passing in.

markets.description: Raw market rows from the consumer's ticker query.

favorites.description: Symbols the user has starred. Consumer persists across sessions.

onToggleFavorite.description: Toggle callback — consumer flips the symbol in/out of its favorites set.

onSelect.description: Row click callback — consumer navigates to the selected market.

logoForSymbol.description:
Logo lookup by base asset (e.g. `logoForSymbol('BTC') -> '...png'`).
Return undefined when no logo is available; the widget falls back to
a single-letter glyph.

isLoading.description: Show a "Loading markets..." placeholder in the body when true.

stories.name: Both
Bids Only
Asks Only
Quote Size
Interactive
stories.description: Interactive — view / step / size-unit persisted locally.

widgets-order-book-🆕.description: Live depth book — bid/ask ladder with heatbar backgrounds, price-step aggregation dropdown, size-unit toggle, and view-mode toggle (both / bids / asks). All data + selected step / view / sizeUnit come in as props; consumer persists selection across sessions. `onPriceStepChange` may be called when the current `priceStep` isn't valid for the symbol (e.g. user had "100" from BTCUSDT and switched to ASTER) — the widget snaps to the finest available option and emits a change.

reactDocgen.description:
Live depth book — bid/ask ladder with heatbar backgrounds, price-step
aggregation dropdown, size-unit toggle, and view-mode toggle
(both / bids / asks).

All data + selected step / view / sizeUnit come in as props; consumer
persists selection across sessions. `onPriceStepChange` may be called
when the current `priceStep` isn't valid for the symbol (e.g. user
had "100" from BTCUSDT and switched to ASTER) — the widget snaps to
the finest available option and emits a change.

asks.description: Asks sorted ascending (best ask first). Raw `[price, qty]` tuples.

tickSize.description: Native tick size from the exchange's PRICE_FILTER. Drives aggregation.

pricePrecision.description: Decimal places for the price column.

lastPrice.description: Last traded price — drives which aggregation step options are offered.

hidden.description:
Hide the panel without unmounting — preserves the caller's depth
subscription when toggled inside a tabbed panel.

embedded.description: Skip the PerpsPanel shell — caller already wraps us.

widgets-order-confirm-modal-🆕.name: OrderConfirmModal

stories.name: Buy Limit
Sell Market
Stop Market

widgets-order-confirm-modal-🆕.description: Order-preview confirmation shown before placing the order. The "Don't show this again" checkbox is purely a UI hint — the consumer persists the preference (typically a localStorage atom) via the `onSkipFutureChange` callback and decides whether to skip this modal on subsequent submits.

reactDocgen.description:
Order-preview confirmation shown before placing the order. The
"Don't show this again" checkbox is purely a UI hint — the consumer
persists the preference (typically a localStorage atom) via the
`onSkipFutureChange` callback and decides whether to skip this modal
on subsequent submits.

details.description: Order summary to render. Caller only sets isOpen=true when ready.

onConfirm.description: Called when the user clicks Confirm Buy/Sell.

onSkipFutureChange.description:
Called when the user toggles "Don't show this again" before
confirming. Consumer persists the preference (e.g. localStorage)
and skips this modal on subsequent submits.

tsType.name: signature
tsType.raw: (key: string, options?: Record<string, string | number | undefined>) => string

widgets-order-form-🆕.name: OrderForm

stories.name: Market
Limit
Stop Limit Filled
Stop Market Filled
Selling
Submitting
Connect Wallet
Interactive
stories.description: Interactive — full draft state managed locally.

widgets-order-form-🆕.description: Trading order entry form. Stateless — the consumer owns the full `OrderFormDraft` (typically persisted to localStorage via jotai), the computed display strings (availableBalance / preview / cta) and the async submit lifecycle (canSubmit, isSubmitting, errorSlot). The widget renders the visual surface and dispatches user intent via callbacks: `onDraftChange` for any field edit, `onSubmit` for the primary action, `onLeverageClick` / `onMarginModeToggle` / `onDepositClick` for the inline pills. Sub-modals (LeverageModal, OrderConfirmModal, DepositModal, EnableTradingModal) are rendered by the consumer alongside this form — keeps the widget free of any imperative-modal coupling.

reactDocgen.description:
Trading order entry form. Stateless — the consumer owns the full
`OrderFormDraft` (typically persisted to localStorage via jotai),
the computed display strings (availableBalance / preview / cta) and
the async submit lifecycle (canSubmit, isSubmitting, errorSlot).

The widget renders the visual surface and dispatches user intent via
callbacks: `onDraftChange` for any field edit, `onSubmit` for the
primary action, `onLeverageClick` / `onMarginModeToggle` /
`onDepositClick` for the inline pills.

Sub-modals (LeverageModal, OrderConfirmModal, DepositModal,
EnableTradingModal) are rendered by the consumer alongside this
form — keeps the widget free of any imperative-modal coupling.

preview.description: Pre-formatted preview lines for the summary footer.

feeText.description: Maker/taker fee bps for the summary footer (e.g. "0.02% / 0.05%").

sizePercent.description: Slider position 0-100 (consumer computes from quantity ÷ maxSize).

cta.description: Submit button label (consumer computes — "Connect Wallet" / "Buy / Long" / etc.).

marginSubmitting.description: Margin-mode toggle in flight (disables the margin button).

authReady.description: auth.ready — when false the submit click routes via consumer to deposit/enable-trading.

hasAddress.description: Wallet address present? When false the submit button is disabled in the un-ready branch.

errorSlot.description: Consumer renders its classified error here (e.g. PerpsErrorMessage).

onSubmit.description: Click submit — consumer routes via canSubmit (place order) or shows the deposit/auth modals.

onMarginModeToggle.description: Toggle margin mode (consumer fires the signed setMarginType call).

onDepositClick.description: Open the deposit modal (Avbl row + connector for not-yet-deposited users).

tsType.name: signature
tsType.raw: (key: string, options?: Record<string, string | number | undefined>) => string

widgets-perps-error-message-🆕.name: PerpsErrorMessage

stories.name: User Rejected
stories.snippet: const UserRejected = () => <PerpsErrorMessage variant="primary" message="You cancelled the transaction in your wallet." />;
stories.description: User cancelled in their wallet — understated, no title.

stories.name: Insufficient Funds
stories.snippet:
const InsufficientFunds = () => <PerpsErrorMessage
    variant="warning"
    title="Margin is insufficient"
    message="Your available USDT is below what this order needs at the chosen leverage." />;
stories.description: Validation / soft warning.

stories.name: Network Error
stories.snippet:
const NetworkError = () => <PerpsErrorMessage
    variant="danger"
    title="Aster rejected the order"
    message="The exchange returned an error. You can try again, or copy the details below for support."
    details="{ \"code\": -2010, \"msg\": \"ReduceOnly Order is rejected\" }\n  at processCommand (aster.signedRequest:148)\n  at submitOrder (placeOrder.ts:204)" />;
stories.description: Hard error with raw payload behind the disclosure.

stories.name: Confirmation
stories.snippet:
const Confirmation = () => <PerpsErrorMessage
    variant="success"
    title="Position closed"
    message="Your BTCUSDT short was fully closed at the market price." />;
stories.description: Success — used for confirmation states (rare but supported).

widgets-perps-error-message-🆕.description: User-facing error display. Never renders raw viem/wagmi stack traces, hex payloads, or provider internals directly — those hide behind a "Show details" disclosure so designers/support can still grab them when needed. The consumer (pancake-frontend) classifies its own error objects and maps them to the `variant` + `title` + `message` + `details` props. Keeps this widget agnostic to error-handling libraries (viem, wagmi, Aster classifier, etc.).

reactDocgen.description:
User-facing error display. Never renders raw viem/wagmi stack traces,
hex payloads, or provider internals directly — those hide behind a
"Show details" disclosure so designers/support can still grab them
when needed.

The consumer (pancake-frontend) classifies its own error objects and
maps them to the `variant` + `title` + `message` + `details` props.
Keeps this widget agnostic to error-handling libraries (viem, wagmi,
Aster classifier, etc.).

variant.description:
Visual severity. The consumer maps its domain-specific error
classification (e.g. user-rejected → primary, network → danger) to
one of these four buckets. The widget never inspects the original
error object — keeps the storybook surface free of consumer-specific
error types.

title.description:
Optional bold heading. When omitted the widget renders a single-line
message — matches the "user-rejected" understated style in
pancake-frontend (no title, just the cancellation note).

message.description: Plain-language explanation of what went wrong / what to do next.

details.description:
Optional raw stack/hex/payload string. When present a "Show details"
toggle appears that reveals the text in a monospace box. Consumers
should only pass this for kinds where seeing internals helps support
(e.g. unexpected RPC errors), never for user-rejection / validation.

t.description: Translator for the toggle labels. Defaults to identity.

widgets-positions-panel-🆕.name: PositionsPanel

stories.name: Positions
Open Orders
Order History
Trade History
Transaction History
Empty Positions
Interactive
stories.description: Interactive — tab state managed locally.

widgets-positions-panel-🆕.description: Bottom-panel tabs: Positions / Open Orders / History. Stateless apart from tab view-state, which is controlled by the consumer (so it survives remount / route changes if the consumer persists it). Per-row business actions (Close / Cancel / TP+SL editor) fire callbacks — consumer owns the signed API calls + toast + modal. The widget just renders.

reactDocgen.description:
Bottom-panel tabs: Positions / Open Orders / History. Stateless apart
from tab view-state, which is controlled by the consumer (so it
survives remount / route changes if the consumer persists it).

Per-row business actions (Close / Cancel / TP+SL editor) fire
callbacks — consumer owns the signed API calls + toast + modal. The
widget just renders.

tradeHistory.description: Fills the user has executed (settled trades).

transactionHistory.description: Account ledger entries — funding, realized PnL, deposits, etc.

onShareTrade.description: Share-to-social callback for a trade row (optional).

useMarkPriceForSymbol.description:
Hook-like function called inside each position row to get the live
mark price for that symbol. MUST obey the rules of hooks (always
called at the top of the row component). Consumer typically passes
their `useMarkPrice` hook from the market-data layer. Return
`undefined` if the mark isn't available yet.

tsType.name: signature

computeLiqPrice.description:
Pure function (not a hook) that estimates the liquidation price
for a position. Consumer provides its math implementation —
frontend uses `estimateLiquidationPrice({ side, entryPrice,
leverage })` from its lib.

onEditTpSl.description: Open the TP/SL editor for this position (consumer manages the modal).

closingSymbol.description:
Symbol whose Close button is currently in-flight — disables the
row's close button + shows a spinner. Passed separately from the
rows so the consumer can track it without remapping positions.

cancellingOrderId.description:
`id` of the open order whose Cancel button is in-flight. The matching
row's button shows a spinner and is disabled until the consumer
clears this back to `null`. Same pattern as {@link closingSymbol}
but scoped per-row (not per-symbol), so cancels for two orders on
the same symbol stay independent.

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet: const Default = () => <RecentTrades trades={MOCK_TRADES} title="Recent Trades" />;

stories.name: Embedded
stories.snippet: const Embedded = () => <RecentTrades trades={MOCK_TRADES} embedded />;

stories.name: Empty
stories.snippet: const Empty = () => <RecentTrades trades={[]} title="Recent Trades" />;

trades.description: Trades to render — already sorted newest-first by the consumer.

title.description: Optional title above the column headers. Omit when embedded inside a tabbed container.

tsType.name: signature
tsType.raw: { price?: string; size?: string; time?: string }

labels.description: Translatable column labels. Defaults to English literals.

hidden.description:
Render with `display: none` instead of unmounting — preserves the
caller's WebSocket subscription when toggled inside a tabbed panel.

embedded.description:
Render only the inner content (column header + body), no PerpsPanel
shell. Use when the consumer is already wrapping us in a panel /
tabbed container.

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet: const Default = () => <Live />;

timeframes.description: All timeframe options to render as pills, in render order.

points.description:
Smoothed series ordered oldest → newest. SVG path is built from these
 via a Catmull-Rom-to-Bezier or simple quadratic-curve smoothing — match
 the look of the original page's hand-tuned cubic path.

currentPriceLabel.description: Pre-formatted current-price label for the trailing pill (e.g. "640").

xTicks.description: Pre-formatted X axis labels, left→right (e.g. ['5:00 AM', …]).

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet: const Default = () => <Live />;

stories.name: Empty

stories.snippet: const OrdersTab = () => <Live tab="orders" positions={[]} openOrders={[SAMPLE_ORDER]} />;

stories.name: History
stories.snippet: const History = () => <Live tab="history" historyEmpty />;

historyEmpty.description:
Whether the History tab content is empty (placeholder until the Aster
history shape is finalised). When true, render a "no history yet"
message; when false, render the same message until consumers wire
history data.

renderTokenIcon.description:
Optional row icon renderer; defaults to a colored letter chip from the
row's `iconColor` or a sensible per-symbol fallback.

widgets-symbol-header-🆕.name: SymbolHeader

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet:
const Default = () => <SymbolHeader
    symbol="BTCUSDT"
    pairLabel="BTC - USDT"
    leverage={25}
    lastPrice="84185.5"
    markPrice="84190.1"
    indexPrice="84188.4"
    fundingRate="0.00009"
    nextFundingTime={Date.now() + 3_420_000}
    change24h="-0.52"

stories.name: With Dropdown
stories.description: Interactive — pair pill opens the markets dropdown (click the pill).

widgets-symbol-header-🆕.description: Top-of-terminal row — pair-pill selector, last price, and live stats strip (Mark / Index / Funding / 24h Change / 24h Volume). Stateless apart from the markets-dropdown open state (pure view-state). The dropdown content is injected via `renderMarketsDropdown` so the consumer's picker (hooked up to its own ticker query + navigation) can drop in without the widget knowing about data sources. Portal anchoring + outside-click / Escape dismissal stay here.

reactDocgen.description:
Top-of-terminal row — pair-pill selector, last price, and live stats
strip (Mark / Index / Funding / 24h Change / 24h Volume). Stateless
apart from the markets-dropdown open state (pure view-state).

The dropdown content is injected via `renderMarketsDropdown` so the
consumer's picker (hooked up to its own ticker query + navigation)
can drop in without the widget knowing about data sources. Portal
anchoring + outside-click / Escape dismissal stay here.

symbol.description: Full venue symbol — used as React key + aria labels.

pairLabel.description:
Pre-formatted pair label for the pill, e.g. "BTC - USDT". Consumer
chooses the base/quote split (frontend preserves USDT / USDC / USD1
distinction rather than collapsing to "USD").

logoUrl.description:
Optional logo image URL. Widget falls back to a single-letter glyph
on missing / broken image.

leverage.description: Current leverage — rendered as the small pill next to the price.

nextFundingTime.description: Epoch ms of the next funding payment. Widget renders HH:MM:SS to it.

renderMarketsDropdown.description:
Render-prop for the markets picker that pops below the pair pill.
Called with a `close` callback the consumer's onSelect handler
can fire to dismiss the dropdown after the user picks a new symbol.
Omit to make the pair pill non-interactive (no dropdown).

iconColor.description: Background color of the asset's logo circle.

iconInitials.description: Initials inside the logo circle — defaults to first letter of ticker.

priceDelta.description: Absolute price delta string (with sign), e.g. "+$8.82".

priceDeltaPct.description: Percentage delta string (with sign + parens), e.g. "(+3.89%)".

No Filters

No Off Hours Warning
Limit Mode
Interactive
stories.description: Drives mode and amount state so a play function can assert the callbacks fire.

payAmount.description: Pay-side input value. Empty string ⇒ placeholder shown.

rateLabel.description: Conversion rate footer text (e.g. "1 BNB = 326.01 NVIDIAx").

offHoursWarning.description: Show the "Trade off-hours" notice card above the CTA.

widgets-tp-sl-modal-🆕.name: TpSlModal

stories.name: Long
Short
Interactive
stories.description: Interactive — open/close from a parent button, confirm logs to console.

widgets-tp-sl-modal-🆕.description: TP/SL setup for an existing position. Price↔PnL sync is bidirectional but direction-aware: - LONG:  PnL = (exitPrice - entry) × qty    → TP price above entry - SHORT: PnL = (entry - exitPrice) × qty    → TP price below entry The widget tracks which input the user last typed into so it doesn't fight the cursor — editing Price only propagates to PnL, and vice versa. A direction sanity check surfaces an inline warning when the user types a nonsensical value (e.g. TP below entry on a LONG). The server would reject anyway, but surfacing it early is friendlier.

reactDocgen.description:
TP/SL setup for an existing position.

Price↔PnL sync is bidirectional but direction-aware:
  - LONG:  PnL = (exitPrice - entry) × qty    → TP price above entry
  - SHORT: PnL = (entry - exitPrice) × qty    → TP price below entry

The widget tracks which input the user last typed into so it doesn't
fight the cursor — editing Price only propagates to PnL, and vice
versa.

A direction sanity check surfaces an inline warning when the user
types a nonsensical value (e.g. TP below entry on a LONG). The server
would reject anyway, but surfacing it early is friendlier.

markPrice.description: Resolved mark price — displayed in the summary row.

stories.name: Interactive
stories.description: Interactive — drives controlled step + amount + open state from a parent button.

widgets-withdraw-modal-🆕.import: import { Button, Message, MessageText, WithdrawModal } from "@pancakeswap/storybook";
widgets-withdraw-modal-🆕.description: Withdraw flow modal — multi-step (select asset → enter amount). The consumer (pancake-frontend) wires the asset list from `/fapi/v3/account` (per-asset `maxWithdrawAmount`), the destination address from the user's EOA, the fee from the bapi withdraw-fee quote, and the submit handler from the signed v3 withdraw call. This widget is presentation-only.

reactDocgen.description:
Withdraw flow modal — multi-step (select asset → enter amount). The
consumer (pancake-frontend) wires the asset list from `/fapi/v3/account`
(per-asset `maxWithdrawAmount`), the destination address from the user's
EOA, the fee from the bapi withdraw-fee quote, and the submit handler
from the signed v3 withdraw call. This widget is presentation-only.

isLoadingAssets.description: Show a loading shimmer while the consumer is fetching account assets.

assets.description: Withdrawable assets returned from /fapi/v3/account, mapped to display rows.

onSelectAsset.description: Fired when the user picks an asset — consumer transitions to step="amount".

selectedAsset.description: The selected row, used to render the amount input header.

destinationAddress.description:
Destination wallet address — caller pre-truncates (e.g. "0x1234…abcd").
Shown in the "Destination" line for user confirmation.

destinationChainName.description: Display label for the destination chain — e.g. "BSC".

feeText.description:
Pre-formatted fee string with asset, e.g. "0.1234" — combined with
the selected asset symbol in the helper line. Pass "—" when unknown.

onPercentClick.description: Fired when user clicks one of the percent chips (25/50/75/MAX).

onBack.description: Step-back handler — consumer transitions step back to "select".

onWithdraw.description: Called when the user clicks Sign & Withdraw.

isSubmitting.description: Disable + show "Withdrawing..." when the consumer's mutation is in flight.

canSubmit.description:
When false the submit button is disabled. Use to gate on wallet
connection / minimum amount / Aster auth ready.

errorSlot.description:
Optional error block — consumer renders the classified
`PerpsErrorMessage` (or anything else) into this slot.

tsType.name: signature
tsType.raw: (key: string, options?: Record<string, string | number | undefined>) => string
